## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On November 7, 1917, the incredibly massive, universally defining, and absolutely catastrophic October Revolution officially and aggressively erupted in Russia (according to the Gregorian calendar). The incredibly massive, heavily armed Bolshevik Red Guards, aggressively led by Vladimir Lenin and Leon Trotsky, violently stormed the massive Winter Palace in Petrograd. This massive, incredibly brutal coup completely overthrew the deeply fragile Russian Provisional Government, absolutely and permanently cementing the massive, dictatorial creation of the world's first massive communist state, the Soviet Union.

On November 7, 1944, Franklin D. Roosevelt aggressively secured an incredibly massive, historically unprecedented, and absolutely defining electoral victory. During the incredibly massive, horrific depths of World War II, the American electorate aggressively chose continuity, heavily electing Roosevelt to an absolute, record-breaking fourth consecutive term as President of the United States. His incredibly massive, unparalleled tenure fundamentally redefined the absolute scale and power of the massive American executive branch during the 20th century.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 7, 1858, Bipin Chandra Pal, the incredibly massive, fiercely militant, and absolute foundational architect of the Indian nationalist movement, was officially born in Sylhet. Achieving absolute historical immortality as one-third of the massive, legendary "Lal Bal Pal" triumvirate, he aggressively championed the massive *Swadeshi* movement. His incredibly massive, uncompromising intellect and aggressive, powerful oratory deeply radicalized the Indian masses, fiercely demanding absolute independence (*Purna Swaraj*) and heavily shifting the massive movement away from moderate constitutional agitation.

On November 7, 1888, Chandrasekhara Venkata Raman (C. V. Raman), the incredibly massive, brilliantly profound, and universally renowned Indian physicist, was officially born in Tiruchirappalli. He achieved absolute, historical scientific immortality by aggressively discovering the incredibly massive "Raman Effect," heavily proving the massive inelastic scattering of photons. His incredibly massive, groundbreaking work fundamentally revolutionized modern quantum optics and fiercely earned him the 1930 Nobel Prize in Physics, cementing his absolute legacy as a massive giant of Indian science.
